• Product features: headline capabilities, recent updates, feature gaps, and how they frame their value proposition. 

Deliverables 
1. A concise slide deck (PowerPoint or Google Slides) highlighting key findings, charts, and takeaways. 
2. A supporting spreadsheet or dashboard (Excel, Google Sheets, or Power BI) with raw data, source links, and side-by-side benchmarks. 
3. A brief summary section recommending quick-win opportunities and longer-term moves our sales & marketing team can act on. 

Acceptance criteria 
• All claims backed by public sources or verifiable data (e.g., website copy, pricing pages, ads, press releases, SimilarWeb, SEMrush). 
• Insights presented in plain language with visuals where useful. 
• Ready to hand over within the agreed timeline so my management meeting next month is fully informed. 

If you excel at turning scattered market data into crisp, strategic insights, I’m ready to move quickly.

Frontend

• Clear status messaging for API latency or errors.

Backend / Integration 
• You hook up to the real-time endpoint (REST or WebSocket—whichever matches the data source you choose). 
• Handle auth keys securely, cache intelligently to avoid rate-limit issues, and keep the calculation cycle under one second.

Deliverables 
• Full source code with comments. 
• Brief setup docs (env variables, build, deploy). 
• Deployed demo link or screen-capture walkthrough proving real-time updates and accurate model output.

Acceptance criteria 
• Landing page loads in <2 s on 4G. 
• Data refreshes continuously without page reload. 
• Model output matches my test values to ±0.5 %. 

If you have experience with high-frequency data, securities APIs, or numerical models, that’s a plus—let me know in your proposal.

full_description:
Title:
Structural Engineer - Australian Compliance for Innovative Volcanic Stone Wall System

Project Description

We are looking for an experienced Australian structural engineer (or engineer with extensive knowledge of the Australian National Construction Code) to assist with introducing an innovative volcanic stone wall system into the Australian construction market.

The product uses natural Armenian tuff stone as an external façade and has been successfully used in Europe and the Middle East. Our goal is to develop an engineered wall system suitable for Australian residential and commercial construction.

The successful engineer will ideally have experience with:

Australian building standards (NCC and relevant Australian Standards)
Masonry, façade or cladding systems
Structural design and engineering certification
Timber and steel framed construction
Wind loading, seismic and structural performance
Building product approvals and compliance pathways

Scope of work

Review the proposed wall system and provide engineering feedback.
Recommend the most suitable fixing system for installation onto timber and/or steel framing.
Determine structural requirements, including load transfer and connection details.
Assess compliance with relevant Australian Standards and the NCC.
Advise on testing required (structural, durability, fire, weatherproofing, etc.).
Prepare structural calculations and engineering documentation where required.
Assist in developing a compliant wall system suitable for certification and future commercial use.

About the Product

Natural volcanic Armenian tuff stone.
Faster installation than traditional brickwork.
Lightweight engineered panel system under development.
Insulated wall assembly designed to improve thermal performance.
Intended to reduce construction time while providing a premium natural stone finish.

Experience with innovative building products, façade systems, precast concrete, insulated panels, masonry, or CodeMark certification would be highly regarded.

Please include examples of similar engineering projects, façade systems, cladding systems, or alternative building materials you've worked on.

• Remove or replace the background cleanly so the subject pops and there are no halos or color fringing.

I usually work with layered PSDs in Adobe Photoshop, but I’m happy to receive 16-bit TIFFs or high-res JPEGs as long as you also share an editable file with all key adjustment layers intact. Color grading should remain consistent across the full batch so the set feels cohesive online and in print.

• Skin retains pore detail and realistic tone. 
• Lighting enhancements are free of banding or obvious dodge-and-burn streaks. 
• Background extractions have clean edges at 200 % zoom and match the new backdrop’s depth and color. 
• Final export is at least 300 dpi, sRGB for web, CMYK on request for print.

Let me know how many portraits you can comfortably deliver per day and show me a couple of before-and-after samples that highlight your fashion retouching chops. Looking forward to collaborating!

Electrical layout

Ventilation system

AutoCAD

## Please Include in Your Proposal:

1. Examples of websites/apps you helped rank.

If you have your own creative suggestions—perhaps subtle motion graphics or a refined title sequence—I’m open to them, provided they enhance the cinematic mood rather than distract.

Send over a reel or a link to past projects that best match this style, and note how soon you could take on the first edit. I’m ready to get started as soon as we find the right fit.

Key features to implement

title: Interactive PC Kart Racing Game
skills: PHYSICS | Physics | UI / User Interface | Game Programming | Unity | Game Development | Game Design
full_description:
I’m building a fast, skill-based arcade kart racer for PC that captures the competitive energy of classics like Crash Team Racing while pushing the track itself into the action. Rounds are won by reaching the finish line first, yet every lap feels different because oversized set-pieces, hazards, timed obstacles, and route shifts actively react to the players on screen.

The core deliverable I need is a playable PC build that supports real-time multiplayer and showcases:

• Tight, responsive kart physics that reward drifting, boosting, and weapon accuracy. 
• Dynamic track technology: hazards that trigger on proximity, alternate paths that open or close, and environmental events synced to in-race timers. 
• Stable online play (peer-to-peer or client-server; I’m open to your suggestion) with a simple matchmaking or lobby flow. 
• Basic UI/UX, audio cues, and placeholder art sufficient to demonstrate the full game loop.

I’m flexible on engine choice as long as you can hit 60 fps on a mid-range gaming PC and leave room for future content drops, cosmetics, and additional modes. Share links to any comparable racing or physics-heavy multiplayer projects you’ve shipped; code architecture and net-sync experience will weigh heavily in my decision.

If you can hand me a clean, well-documented project folder plus a build that lets four friends jump in, race, and feel the track fight back, let’s get moving.
